name: Lemon Walnut Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sugar, cocoa powder, and salt in a large, heavy-bottomed saucepan. Stir to mix and break up any lumps of cocoa powder.
2. Add the corn syrup, milk, and 2 tablespoons of butter. Place over medium heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil.
3. Remove from heat; cover the pan with a tight-fitting lid for about one minute to steam off any sugar crystals on the sides of the pan. Watch closely so the fudge does not boil over.
4. Uncover and continue to cook (without stirring), swabbing down the sides of the pan occasionally with a pastry brush dipped in water, until the temperature reaches the soft-ball stage.
- Use a candy thermometer or the old-fashioned method: drop a bit of hot candy into cold water. When it forms a cohesive puddle on the bottom of the cup and can be formed into a ball that flattens when lifted out of the water, it's at the soft-ball stage.
5. Immediately swab down the sides of the pan. Pour (do not scrape) the fudge onto a slab of marble or a large platter or into a flat baking dish.
6. Heat the lemon zest, juice, and vanilla extract to boiling and pour over the hot candy. Top with the remaining 2 tablespoons butter. Let the mixture cool to room temperature without disturbing it.
7. Stir the fudge with a spatula or plastic scraper until it becomes thick and starts to lose its gloss.
8. Add the chopped nuts just as the fudge becomes too stiff to stir.
9. Spread the fudge onto a buttered plate or sheet of plastic wrap. After a few minutes, it will solidify and you can wrap and store it for several days—up to weeks if kept secret! Yields 2-1/8 lbs.
Per 3-oz serving: 340 calories, 4 g protein, 62 g carbohydrate, 10 g fat, 21 mg cholesterol, 74 mg sodium, 1 g fiber.
